steps.title: Read the key data points
steps.content: The table covers 1910 to 1925. In 1910: 123,990 cars produced, 320 companies. In 1925: 3,185,881 cars produced, 80 companies. Production rose sharply; the number of companies fell sharply. A correct choice must capture both movements.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the number of cars produced increased but the number of companies producing cars decreased.': Accurately captures both trends — production up, companies down. ✓
  • Choice B — 'both the number of cars produced and the number of companies producing cars remained unchanged.': Both figures changed dramatically; neither remained unchanged. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the number of cars produced decreased but the number of companies producing cars remained unchanged.': Production nearly tripled; it did not decrease. Companies also did not remain unchanged. ✗
  • Choice D — 'both the number of cars produced and the number of companies producing cars increased.': The number of companies fell from 320 to 80 — it did not increase. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
